name: compose
description: Single entrypoint for deterministic orchestration with Stage x Policy x Lens from explicit macro syntax. English triggers: compose, composition, orchestration, workflow fusion.
purpose: Parse explicit skill macros into one deterministic execution program without adding hidden domain logic.
when_to_use: Need to normalize a multi-skill macro into one deterministic execution plan.
Need workflow expansion from registry-backed workflow skills.
Need stage, lens, scope, or output overrides resolved before domain execution.
do_not_use: Need domain analysis, implementation, review, or debugging by itself.
Need shorthand or non-canonical names instead of explicit workflows or explicit skills.
Need to infer missing domain skills without an explicit macro or workflow skill.
Need direct PROGRAM DSL input parsing as a public entry mode.

required_inputs.name: SCOPE_OVERRIDE
required_inputs.description: Optional explicit scope tokens such as @src or scope:paths(src/auth).

input_contract_notes: Public compose entry is macro-only. Direct PROGRAM DSL parsing is internal and not part of the public skill contract.
Bracket payloads `[ ... ]` are appended to prompt tail in encounter order and are treated as explicit prompt text.
Scope can come from `@path`, markdown doc tokens, or explicit `scope:...`; compose only normalizes what is explicitly present.

structured_outputs.name: INPUT_MODE
structured_outputs.description: Public input mode used for the current parse.

structured_outputs.name: PARSED_SKILLS
structured_outputs.description: Explicit skills parsed from the macro before workflow expansion.

structured_outputs.name: EFFECTIVE_SKILLS
structured_outputs.description: Explicit skills after internal response-layer normalization but before workflow flattening.

structured_outputs.name: EXPANDED_SKILLS
structured_outputs.description: Deterministically flattened skill list after workflow expansion.

structured_outputs.name: PARSED_DOC_INPUTS
structured_outputs.description: Document or path tokens parsed from the macro.

structured_outputs.name: PROMPT_TAIL
structured_outputs.description: Concatenated explicit prompt payload parsed from free text and bracket blocks.

structured_outputs.name: NORMALIZED_SCOPE
structured_outputs.description: Resolved scope after macro tokens and skill defaults are merged.

structured_outputs.name: LENS_SOURCE
structured_outputs.description: Where the final lens came from.

structured_outputs.name: RESPONSE_PROFILE
structured_outputs.description: Resolved response profile metadata for the final render.

structured_outputs.name: STRUCTURAL_WARNINGS
structured_outputs.description: Non-blocking orchestration warnings such as duplicate collapse or implicit response-layer append.

structured_outputs.name: STRUCTURAL_ERRORS
structured_outputs.description: Blocking parse or expansion errors returned when macro normalization fails.

output_contract_notes: PROGRAM is the normalized one-line DSL string; the parser may also emit an auxiliary structured program breakdown.
PARSED_SKILLS preserves the user macro surface, while EFFECTIVE_SKILLS includes internal response-layer normalization and EXPANDED_SKILLS removes workflow names.
When normalization fails, STRUCTURAL_ERRORS should explain the blocking reason instead of guessing hidden fallback behavior.
neutrality_rules: Parse only explicit macro, registry, and override inputs.
Do not invent domain conclusions, findings, or hidden stage logic during orchestration.
If a workflow expansion is invalid or cyclic, return the structural error instead of guessing a fallback.
execution_constraints: Do not perform domain analysis, implementation, or verification inside compose.
Collapse duplicate skills only after workflow expansion and report the collapse as a warning.
If two or more explicit skills remain in the macro, final response profile resolves to the generic composite profile.
mandatory_rules: Prefer canonical wf-* workflow names in new macros.
